task_completion.description: The OpenICE Multiparameter Monitor simulator and Vital Signs clinical application are both open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Windows for both the Multiparameter Monitor simulator and the Vital Signs application are visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The Heart Rate (HR) is set to 120 BPM in the Multiparameter Monitor simulator controls.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The control panel or settings window for the simulator shows the Heart Rate parameter explicitly set to 120.

task_completion.description: The Vital Signs application correctly reflects the 120 BPM Heart Rate setting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Signs app display shows a Heart Rate (HR) reading of 120.

task_completion.description: A screenshot showing both the device controls and the Vital Signs app at 120 BPM is saved to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'calibration_120bpm.png' is visible on the Desktop or in a file manager window for /home/ga/Desktop/.

task_completion.description: A CSV report is created with the required columns and test points (60, 90, 120 BPM).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'calibration_report.csv' is visible on the Desktop, and when opened in a text editor or spreadsheet, it contains the columns 'Test_Point', 'Input_Setting_BPM', 'Observed_Display_BPM', 'Pass_Fail' with rows corresponding to the 60, 90, and 120 BPM test points.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenICE GUI applications to change and observe the heart rate.
integrity.visual_evidence: Trajectory shows the agent clicking or typing within the Multiparameter Monitor and Vital Signs application windows, rather than just using terminal commands to spoof the state.

integrity.description: The CSV report was generated based on actual task execution rather than pre-written scripts bypassing the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen opening a text editor, spreadsheet, or using echo/cat commands in the terminal to write the CSV after observing the apps, rather than creating the file before opening the simulator.

integrity.description: The screenshot was actively taken by the agent during the 120 BPM test point.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses a screenshot tool (like gnome-screenshot or a keyboard shortcut) while both the simulator controls and Vital Signs app are visible and showing 120 BPM.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The calibration report must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/calibration_report.csv. The evidence screenshot must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/calibration_120bpm.png. The task requires testing at three standard medical device test points: 60, 90, and 120 BPM. The final verification screenshot must show the HR set to 120 BPM on both the simulator controls and the Vital Signs app.
